A list of the weapons is here: Clear the RAM on an iPad or iPhone Follow these steps to clear the Random Access Memory (RAM) on your iPad or iPhone: 1. **Open the App Switcher:** The App Switcher is a feature that allows you to switch between open apps on your device. To access it, quickly press the Home button twice on iPhones with a Home button (like the iPhone 8 or earlier), or swipe up from the bottom of the screen and pause midway on devices without a Home button (like the iPhone X or later, and all iPads). This action will bring up the App Switcher, which displays previews of all currently running apps. *Key tip:* Make sure you're using the correct gesture for your specific device model. If you have trouble remembering the gestures, practice them until they become second nature. 2. **Locate the app you want to close:** Scroll through the list of open apps until you find the one you wish to force quit. It's important to note that closing apps unnecessarily can actually slow down your device due to increased boot-up time when reopening the apps; however, if an app is misbehaving or causing issues, it may be necessary to force quit it. 3. **Swipe up on the app preview:** Once you've located the app you want to close, place your finger on its preview and swipe upward. This motion should remove the app preview from the App Switcher, effectively terminating the app and freeing up any allocated resources like RAM. 4. **Repeat the process for other apps:** Continue swiping up on additional app previews as needed. Remember that this method only closes individual apps and doesn't directly clear the overall system RAM. However, by closing resource-intensive apps, you indirectly help manage available memory since those closed apps won't continue consuming RAM. 5. **Return to the home screen:** After closing unwanted apps, tap anywhere outside the App Switcher area to dismiss it and return to the home screen. At this point, some people might believe their device's RAM has been cleared, but unfortunately, there isn't a direct way to manually clear the entire system RAM on iOS devices like iPads and iPhones. Instead, rely on Apple's built-in optimizations to manage background processes and allocate RAM efficiently. 6. **Restart your device periodically:** As mentioned before, iOS handles RAM management automatically, so manual intervention usually isn't required. However, restarting your device every few days can still be beneficial. A fresh start ensures no rogue processes are hogging resources and clears out temporary files that could otherwise accumulate over time. Restarting also gives your device's hardware components-including its RAM-a brief respite, potentially improving performance and battery life. 7. **Monitor your device's storage usage:** While not directly related to clearing RAM, keeping tabs on your device's storage usage can help maintain optimal performance. Go to Settings > General > iPad Storage (or iPhone Storage) to view a breakdown of what's taking up space on your device. From here, consider offloading unused apps, deleting unnecessary files, or transferring data to external storage solutions like iCloud or a computer. By doing so, you ensure your device always has enough breathing room to perform at its best. And she just recently has really been interested in um... what's at the very end of your face? Many galaxies continue to form new generations of stars. The Milky Way, and all spiral shaped galaxies like it (see right side image of NGC 2997), produce new stars at a rate of one or two stars per year. This star formation happens in the vast interstellar clouds that account for about 1% to 10% of the mass of these galaxies. Globular star clusters, on the other hand, are not currently forming stars because this activity happened billions of years ago and then stopped once all of the gas and dust clouds were used up. Don't even have a good hard floor to bang your feet on, do you. Covering an ear piercing before swimming is essential to prevent waterborne bacteria from entering the wound and causing infection. This tutorial will provide you with a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to cover your ear piercing effectively using a bandage or waterproof dressing. **Things You'll Need:** 1. Clean hands or gloves 2. Soap and warm water 3. A clean cloth or paper towel 4. Waterproof adhesive bandages (such as Nexcare) or waterproof dressings (such as Tegaderm) 5. Scissors (if necessary) ### **Step 1: Wash Your Hands** Before starting the process, ensure that your hands are clean to avoid introducing any germs or bacteria into the pierced area. Use soap and warm water, lathering up for at least 20 seconds, then rinse thoroughly and dry off with a clean towel or paper towel. Alternatively, wear medical gloves if available. *Key Tip:* Always wash your hands before handling a fresh piercing to minimize the risk of infection. ### **Step 2: Clean the Piercing Area** Gently clean around the piercing site using mild soap and warm water. Pat the area dry with a clean cloth or paper towel; do not rub, as this may irritate the skin further. Make sure the area is completely dry before proceeding to the next step. *Guideline:* Avoid using harsh cleansers, alcohol, or hydrogen peroxide, which can cause tissue damage and delay healing. ### **Step 3: Select Appropriate Bandage or Dressing** Choose a waterproof adhesive bandage or dressing specifically designed to protect wounds from water exposure. These products typically feature a flexible, breathable material backed by an adhesive layer that forms a barrier against moisture. Examples include Nexcare waterproof bandages and Tegaderm transparent film dressings. *Key Tip:* Opt for a larger size than needed to accommodate movement and ensure proper coverage during swimming activities. ### **Step 4: Apply the Bandage or Dressing** Carefully remove the protective covering from one side of the bandage or dressing, exposing the adhesive surface. Position the bandage over the piercing area so that it covers both the front and back portions of the earlobe. Press firmly along all edges to create a secure seal. If using a Tegaderm dressing, cut a hole slightly smaller than the piercing using scissors before applying it. Remove the backing from the dressing and place it centered over the piercing, ensuring good contact between the wound and the clear adhesive surface. Smooth out any air bubbles to improve adherence. *Guideline:* When applying the bandage or dressing, make sure there are no gaps where water could potentially seep through. ### **Step 5: Check Secureness Before Swimming** Test the security of the bandage or dressing by gently tugging at its edges. If it lifts easily or feels loose, replace it with a new one. It should be snug but not too tight, allowing for comfortable movement while still providing adequate protection. *Key Tip:* Replace the bandage or dressing after swimming sessions to maintain hygiene and reduce the risk of infection. By following these steps carefully, you can successfully cover your ear piercing for swimming and significantly decrease the chances of developing an infection due to water exposure. Remember always to prioritize cleanliness and take extra precautions when caring for fresh body modifications like piercings. All of Russia began to use Russian as the language of literature, education, and official communication. Until the 20th century, only the upper classes and people in cities spoke the literary language. Russians from the countryside continued to speak their local dialects. In the 20th century, all children were required to go to school. Many people had radios and televisions, which helped to spread Standard Russian. By the mid-20th century, Russian dialects had mostly disappeared. Standard Russian replaced them almost completely especially to talk to people from different oblasts. Whenever an election comes round, you'll see that people who voted have an ink mark on their fingers. Why do they get it, and why does it not rub off? Keeping track of who voted In a democratic country, citizens get to vote in an election to choose their rulers. As all citizens are equal, each citizen is allowed to vote only once in an election. But how does one make sure someone doesn't cheat and votes more than once? A simple way is to mark everyone who has voted. Then the mark will show and the person cannot vote again. But what kind of mark does one put? A mark that rubs off quickly is useless. But it must go off in the time the next election comes, so that the voter can vote again. So what we need is a mark cannot be rubbed off for sometime, but will wear off by the time the next election comes. What chemical can make a mark like that? Have an election in class to choose your class leader. Use a piggy bank as the ballot box, and make small squares of paper for the ballots. Everyone has to secretly write the name of the person they want to choose as class leader, fold the paper and put it in the ballot box. Ask your chemistry teacher to help you with a little bit of silver nitrate solution from the lab. After each person has voted, dip a glass capillary into the silver nitrate and put a dot on their finger. Hold the finger till it dries. When everyone has voted, you can open the ballot box and count who got the most votes. Why Silver Nitrate Silver nitrate is just the chemical we need. It is soluble in water, so you can make an inky black solution. It comes in bottles with a brush in the lid (like nail polish bottles). When you go to vote the first time, you'll see the election officer draw a thin line over your index finger and its fingernail with the tiny brush. Then they'll hold your finger till the ink dries, giving you a violet mark. When it is put on skin, it reacts with the salt present on it to form silver chloride. Silver chloride is not soluble in water, and clings to your skin. It cannot be washed off with soap and water. Not even hot water. Not even if you use alcohol, nail polish remover, or bleach. (But please don't try these things, they are dangerous.) But as new skin grows and the old skin sloughs off, the ink stain will disappear. The ink on the skin goes off in a week. The ink on the nail takes longer, as the nail grows out. The ink only works if it dries. If you rub it off while still wet, it will go off. That's because I didn't even get the spoon. Freezing columns in Google Sheets can be incredibly useful when working with large datasets, as it allows you to keep important information visible while scrolling through your spreadsheet. This is especially helpful on smaller screens like those found on most Android devices. Here's an easy-to-follow, step-by-step guide on freezing a column in Google Sheets using an Android device: 1. Open the Google Sheets app on your Android device: The first step would be to locate the Google Sheets app on your phone or tablet and open it up. If you don't have the app yet, download it from the Google Play Store. 2. Load your document: Once you are inside the Google Sheets application, browse and select the specific file that requires having a frozen column. Tap on its title to load the file within the app. 3. Locate the column(s) to freeze: Identify which particular column needs to remain fixed at all times during scrolling. Commonly, users prefer freezing their first column (Column A), but this method works for any given vertical section of data within the sheet. 4. Navigate to the 'View' tab: At the top right corner of the screen, there will be three dots representing more options; tap these to reveal additional features available in the app. From the expanded menu, find and select "Gridlines" followed by "View gridlines." This action displays lines between every cell in the dataset making navigation easier. 5. Access the 'Freeze' option: After enabling the visibility of gridlines, look towards the bottom edge of your screen where several icons related to display settings appear. One such icon includes two overlapping rectangles-one small and one larger rectangle stacked vertically over the other. That's the 'Freeze' button! 6. Choose the number of rows to freeze: Upon tapping the 'Freeze' button, you get presented with a few choices depending upon the position of your active cursor - either one row above the current cell, multiple rows, or even entire sheets if required. For our purpose, we want to freeze only a single column, so go ahead and choose the option called "Up to current column." 7. Verify the result: Having applied the freeze command, verify whether the intended column remains locked into place once you start scrolling downwards through the rest of your data. You should notice that no matter how far you descend, the specified column stays stationary, ensuring constant availability of reference data. 8. Tips & Guidelines: * Remember that freezing applies per sheet basis, meaning if you switch tabs or navigate away from the currently opened sheet, you may need to reapply the freeze function accordingly based on your new location. * To unfreeze columns or rows, simply return to Step 5 and deselect the previously chosen freezing configuration. * When dealing with wide tables containing many horizontal fields, consider freezing both leftmost and rightmost significant columns to maintain better contextual awareness whilst navigating horizontally across vast expanses of cells. * Keep in mind that excessive freezing could lead to limited viewability, particularly on mobile platforms; therefore, judicious selection of columns or rows to pin is crucial. Congratulations! By following these clear cut steps, you now know exactly how to freeze a column in Google Sheets via an Android device, thereby enhancing productivity and streamlining user experience. Guitar, plucked stringed musical instrument that probably originated in Spain early in the 16th century, deriving from the guitarra latina, a late-medieval instrument with a waisted body and four strings. The early guitar was narrower and deeper than the modern guitar, with a less pronounced waist. It was closely related to the vihuela, the guitar-shaped instrument played in Spain in place of the lute. = = = children/CHIDES_NA/Parent Child/SLI/PCM/mey21.cha = = = Okay very good Ross I love it. Berlin is an important city for the history of Germany. The King of Prussia and the Emperor of Germany lived in Berlin. The government of Germany was in Berlin for many years. Title: Cook During a Power Outage A power outage can be an inconvenient situation, especially when you have food that needs to be cooked. However, with some basic knowledge and preparation, you can still cook meals during a power outage. This tutorial will guide you through various methods to cook your food safely and efficiently without electricity. **Step 1: Prepare Ahead of Time** The best way to ensure success is by being prepared before a power outage occurs. Here are some key steps for advanced preparation: * Keep a supply of non-perishable foods (like canned goods, dried fruits, nuts) that don't require cooking. * Have manual can openers available. * Store matches or lighters for fire-based cooking methods. * Invest in camping equipment such as portable stoves, fuel, coolers, ice packs, etc., which could prove useful during extended power outages. **Step 2: Use a Barbeque Grill Safely** Barbeque grills are excellent tools for outdoor cooking during a power outage. Follow these safety measures while using one: * Only use approved fuels (charcoal briquettes, propane) according to manufacturer instructions. * Never bring a grill inside your home or garage due to risk of carbon monoxide poisoning. * Make sure the grill is stable and located away from structures, trees, and overhangs. * Clean the grill regularly to prevent grease fires. **Step 3: Cook Over an Open Fire** If you don't have access to a grill, consider building a small fire outside if conditions allow it. Remember these precautions: * Check local regulations regarding open fires. * Choose a safe spot away from buildings, vegetation, and other flammable materials. * Gather dry wood; avoid green or wet wood that creates excessive smoke. * Build a tepee structure with kindling at the base, then gradually add larger pieces of wood around it. * Always extinguish the fire completely when done. **Step 4: Utilize a Portable Stove** Portable stoves provide another option for indoor cooking. Be aware of these guidelines: * Place the stove on a flat, stable surface. * Ensure there's adequate ventilation to avoid carbon monoxide buildup. * Regularly check the condition of the stove and replace parts as needed. * Refuel the stove outside, following manufacturer directions carefully. **Step 5: Preserve Perishables** During a prolonged outage, keep perishable items cold to prevent spoilage. Here's how: * Pack refrigerated items together tightly to maintain coolness. * Avoid opening the fridge door unnecessarily. * Use block ice or reusable freezer gel packs in coolers. * If possible, move frozen items to a separate working freezer or share them with neighbors. **Step 6: Maintain Food Safety** Foodborne illnesses increase chances during power outages since proper storage temperatures may not be maintained. Observe these rules: * Discard any potentially hazardous food after four hours at room temperature. * Throw away foods that show signs of contamination (moldy odor, sliminess, discoloration). * When in doubt, throw it out! It's better to err on the side of caution than risk getting sick. By understanding these steps, you'll feel more confident about preparing meals during a power outage. Just remember - safety should always come first! Well you should not have watched it. Some animals also can use music. Songbirds use song to protect their territory, or to attract a mate. Monkeys have been seen beating hollow logs. This may, of course, also serve to defend the territory. A seismograph is the device that scientists use to measure earthquakes. The goal of a seismograph is to accurately record the motion of the ground during a quake. If you live in a city, you may have noticed that buildings sometimes shake when a big truck or a subway train rolls by. Good seismographs are therefore isolated and connected to bedrock to prevent this sort of "data pollution." The main problem that must be solved in creating a seismograph is that when the ground shakes, so does the instrument. Therefore, most seismographs involve a large mass of some sort. You could make a very simple seismograph by hanging a large weight from a rope over a table. By attaching a pen to the weight and taping a piece of paper to the table so that the pen can draw on the paper, you could record tremors in the Earth's crust (earthquakes). If you used a roll of paper and a motor that slowly pulled the paper across the table, you would be able to record tremors over time. However, it would take a pretty large tremor for you to see anything. In a real seismograph, levers or electronics are used to magnify the signal so that very small tremors are detectable. A big mechanical seismograph may have a weight attached that weighs 1,000 pounds (450 kg) or more, and it drives a set of levers that significantly magnify the pen's motion. The Richter scale is a standard scale used to compare earthquakes. It is a logarithmic scale, meaning that the numbers on the scale measure factors of 10. So, for example, an earthquake that measures 4.0 on the Richter scale is 10 times larger than one that measures 3.0. On the Richter scale, anything below 2.0 is undetectable to a normal person and is called a microquake. Microquakes occur constantly. Moderate earthquakes measure less than 6.0 or so on the Richter scale. Earthquakes measuring more than 6.0 can cause significant damage. The biggest quake in the world since 1900 scored a 9.5 on the Richter scale. It rocked Chile on May 22, 1960. An' now you just've shut up. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. This lesson printed from: In this lesson you will: - Discuss food items you consume for breakfast. - Investigate elements of foreign culture, particularly food. - Use map skills to locate selected foreign nations. - Increase your knowledge of the these definitions: - Economic wants are things people would like to have. - Goods and services are provided to fulfill these wants. - Those who create the goods and provide the services are producers. - Those who use them are consumers. - Consumers found in different parts of the world may have differing wants. - Foods for satisfying hunger are a common economic want. - Foods can vary greatly from one part of the world to another. 1. Your teacher will divide you into cooperative learning groups. (Think about how your multiple intelligences can help the group during this lesson.) Place the square paper in the center of your work space. Everyone should be able to write on it at the same time. Think about the foods you eat for breakfast. Write down your favorite. USE A PENCIL! Talk about these foods in your group. Which are the same? Be ready to share these ideas with the whole class. 2. In the small group, talk about the ingredients used to produce these foods. For example: Cereal from corn or rice, raisins from grapes, jelly from fruit, toast from bread made from wheat. Write INGREDIENTS with CRAYON on your square near your food list. Look at the vocabulary words for today. Read the words from the chart. Can you find a word on your square paper that names a CONSUMER? Write CONSUMER with a MARKER near that word. Can you find a sample of GOODS? Write GOODS with a MARKER near that word on your square paper. Can you find others? Some samples for the key words for today may not be found on your square paper. Choose labels carefully. Using the web site Breakfast Around the World , explore the following countries and decide what would be the basic breakfast food that you would find there. Enter the description of the food in the space provide. Once you have completed this form then go to the following interactive activity and discover how well you research the breakfast foods of these countries. Match the breakfast foods with the correct country. What nation would you expect to have spaghetti for breakfast? 4. Your teacher will assign each group a country. After reading about your country at the web site, choose someone to locate your nation on the classroom wall map. Be prepared to share something interesting about the country with your classmates. You may also wish to find your nation using the site "National Geographic Xpeditions Atlas ." 5. Think to yourself, "Why do you think people in countries around the world eat different foods?" Ask each person in your group to share their ideas. Do you think climate might have something to do with differences? Have you heard of all these different foods? Use a dictionary to check the meaning of a food that you have never heard of. 6. Think about and then share examples of foods that you have eaten that may have come from foreign countries. Do restaurants in your town serve any of these foods? Does the school cafeteria cook foods from nations around the world? 8. As the lesson closes, review the words of the day posted on the charts in your room. Talk about them in your group. Does everyone understand the meanings? Can you give examples of each? Title: How to Activate Edge (EGPRS) on an iPhone Edge, also known as Enhanced Data Rates for Global Evolution or EGPRS, is a technology that allows your iPhone to connect to the internet at faster speeds than with GPRS (General Packet Radio Service). While many iPhones now support 4G LTE or even 5G, activating Edge can still be useful when you find yourself in areas with poor network coverage. This tutorial will guide you through enabling Edge on your iPhone in simple steps. **Step 1:** Access Settings *Navigate to the home screen of your iPhone.* *Locate the 'Settings' app, which has a grey gear icon.* *Tap on the 'Settings' app to open it.* Accessing settings gives you control over various features and configurations of your device. It enables you to customize your iPhone according to your preferences, including managing connections such as mobile data. **Step 2:** Select Mobile Data *In the main menu of 'Settings', scroll down until you see the option labeled 'Mobile Data'. Tap on this option.* Here, you manage all aspects related to cellular data usage. You may monitor your consumption statistics, enable or disable specific apps from using background data, and configure other relevant options like Cellular Data Options. **Step 3:** Manage Cellular Data Network *After tapping on 'Mobile Data', look towards the bottom of the next page where you should find 'Cellular Data Options'. Click on this option.* Under 'Cellular Data Options', there are two sections - 'Data Roaming' and 'Voice & Data'. Here, we want to focus on the latter section. **Step 4:** Change Voice & Data Setting *Within the 'Cellular Data Options' page, tap on 'Voice & Data'. A list of available connection types appears, typically starting from the fastest to slowest: 5G Auto, 5G, LTE, 3G, 2G, and finally EDGE.* By default, your iPhone likely selects one of the higher-speed options based on availability in your area. However, if these aren't working correctly or if they're unavailable, changing the setting to 'EDGE' could help establish a stable connection. **Key Tips:** - Remember that while switching to EDGE might provide better signal strength in weak coverage zones, it won't offer high-speed browsing experience compared to newer technologies like 4G LTE or 5G. - Always switch back to a faster connection once you no longer need EDGE. Leaving your phone set to slower connections unnecessarily hampers user experience due to sluggish loading times. **Guidelines:** - If you often travel internationally, consider disabling 'Data Roaming' under 'Cellular Data Options' to avoid unexpected charges from your carrier. - Keep checking for software updates regularly; these frequently include bug fixes and enhancements that improve overall performance, particularly regarding network connectivity issues. Following these clear, easy-to-understand instructions should allow anyone to activate Edge on their iPhone without any trouble. As always, remember to adjust settings according to your needs, keeping in mind factors like location, network quality, and battery life. I don't know he's just drivin' around. Title: Shapes and Patterns in Three-Dimensional Spaces Hello young explorers! Today, let's dive into an exciting world of shapes and patterns found in three-dimensional spaces. Have you ever played with building blocks or tried to create intricate origami structures? If so, you already have some experience with topology - the study of properties that remain unchanged when shapes are stretched, twisted, or deformed without tearing or gluing them. In higher dimensions, there exist fascinating concepts called "three-manifolds." These complex entities resemble our familiar three-dimensional space but come with unique characteristics that mathematicians love to explore. A long time ago, people thought there were only a handful of these shapes, but now we know there are countless possibilities! Let us focus on specific types of three-manifolds known as "hyperbolic," where every shape looks like it has negative curvature, just like the surface of a saddle. Imagine trying to balance a ball on one of those saddles - interesting, isn't it? Now, here comes the real question: How do we determine whether two different hyperbolic three-manifolds are actually the same, except for being transformed somehow? This problem brings us to the Lins-Lins Challenge mentioned in the text you provided. They asked if two particular hyperbolic three-manifolds created through specific operations (called Dehn surgeries) are identical or not. Mathematicians haven't figured out yet if these two shapes are simply mirror images of each other or fundamentally distinct. Although you might find it challenging to visualize or work with such high dimensional objects yourself, remember that math is all around us, including in your daily life and activities. Playing with tangrams, solving rubik's cubes, creating mazes, and discovering geometric patterns will strengthen your spatial reasoning skills and appreciation for more advanced mathematical ideas like low dimensional topology. Happy exploring! Title: How to Deal with Criticism of Being "Emo" Introduction: Being labeled as "emo" can be hurtful and alienating, especially if you identify with the subculture or are simply an emotionally sensitive person. It's important to remember that everyone has the right to express themselves and their emotions without fear of judgment or criticism. This guide will provide you with steps to help handle criticism about being emo and maintain your self-confidence. Step 1: Understand the term "Emo": Emo is short for 'emotional hardcore,' which originated from a style of music and evolved into a subculture characterized by emotional intensity, sensitivity, and self-expression through fashion, art, and music. Unfortunately, the term has been misused and stereotyped over time, often used pejoratively to describe someone who appears moody, introverted, or overly sensitive. Educate yourself on its origins and meaning to better understand any criticisms directed towards you. Key tip: Remember that labels don't define you; your character does. Step 2: Analyze the criticism: Consider whether there is any truth in the criticism. Are they pointing out something genuine, like excessive sadness? Or is it merely a negative label based on appearance or preconceived ideas? If there is validity in what they say, take this opportunity for introspection and growth. Otherwise, recognize that these comments may reflect more about the person making them than about you. Guideline: Keep an open mind but remain true to yourself. Step 3: Respond calmly and assertively: Responding defensively could reinforce the negative perception. Instead, respond calmly and confidently by acknowledging your feelings and standing up for yourself. For example, "I appreciate your concern, but I prefer to express myself openly." Key tip: Practice active listening skills to show respect while staying firm in your beliefs. Step 4: Avoid taking it personally: Recognize that people might project their own insecurities onto you when using terms like "emo". Try not to internalize such remarks and remind yourself that everyone has unique ways of dealing with emotions. Guideline: Focus on improving your understanding of others rather than reacting impulsively. Step 5: Surround yourself with positivity: Spend time with those who accept and support you for who you are. They will boost your confidence and encourage personal growth beyond societal expectations. Key tip: Cultivate healthy relationships where communication flows freely and constructively. Step 6: Engage in positive activities: Channel your energy into hobbies or creative pursuits that bring joy and fulfillment. This will improve mental health and build resilience against external judgments. Guideline: Make time for enjoyable activities to foster happiness and balance in life. Step 7: Seek professional advice if needed: If critical feedback affects your wellbeing significantly, consider speaking with a therapist or counselor. These professionals can offer strategies for coping with negativity and building self-esteem. Key tip: Prioritize mental health care as part of overall self-care routine. Conclusion: Handling criticism about being "emo" requires patience, understanding, and self-awareness. By following these steps, you'll develop strong coping mechanisms and learn to stand tall amidst adversity. Always remember, embracing one's emotions and individuality should never warrant disdain or mockery. The Commission operates like a cabinet government. There is one Commissioner per member state, though Commissioners are bound to represent the interests of the EU as a whole rather than their home state. If it's nice weather on St. Swithin's Day, it will be fair for 40 days after. This proverb goes back at least to 1599. Description: You have a choice between four configurations (definitions : Like/Unlike refer to the signs of the charges - Equal/Unequal refer to the magnitudes - so Like+Unequal would be two same-signed charges with different magnitudes). In the top window, the two charges are shown on the X axis, and the "test charge" (a small positive charge) is shown between them. There is a vector illustrated above the test charge (it is offset from the axis to make it easier to see) - that is the net force on the test charge from the other two charges. The directions will either be to the left or to the right (remember, this test charge is confined to the x axis - so this is only the Fx component). You can move the charge anywhere along the x axis to see the effect on the net force vector. The bottom window shows the plot of the net force along the x axis for the test charge. The magnitude of the arrow in the top window is plotted along the vertical axis of the bottom window. Also, the convention is, if the force vector (the arrow) points to the left, it is plotted positive in the graph, if it points to the right, it is plotted negative in the graph. Things to try : a) Choose LIKE EQUAL charges - put the test charge a little bit away from the origin (between the charges) .. what happens to it? How about UNLIKE EQUAL - can you find a similar motion? (What about UNLIKE UNEQUAL .. Is this motion as symmetric as the LIKE EQUAL case?) b) Choose one of the UNLIKE systems - can you place the test charge in between the two and get that motion from a) and b)? - If not, why not? a) How does the Like Unequal graph differ from the Like Equal ? (Graph 1 vs Graph 2) b) How does the Like Unequal graph differ from the UnLike Unequal ? (Graph 1 vs Graph 3) c) How does the Unlike Equal graph differ from the Like Equal ? (Graph 4 vs Graph 2) d) How does the Unlike Equal graph differ from the UnLike Unequal ? (Graph 4 vs Graph 3) Extra feature .. animation! If you put the test charge somewhere in the system, you can click the RESTART/PLAY button to "release" the test charge from rest - it will be acted upon by the other charges and move toward or away, depending on the net force on the test charge (if it hits one of the charges, there will be a "collision" and the animation will stop). Now, using that RESTART/PLAY button will start the test charge from rest! If you want to pause the motion, and not move the charge, then continue that same motion, use the RESUME FROM PAUSE button. [See, I found that if you move the charge to a new place, you want to start it from rest .. otherwise the applet would keep the old velocity from the pause - which caused weird, non-physical behavior. So, if you move the charge - use the RESTART/PLAY button ... if you just pause the motion, and then want to continue that motion without you adjusting the test charge .. use the RESUME FROM PAUSE.] Hey, my charge disappeared! If you test charge flies off the screen to either side, or if it collides with one of the fixed charges - use the RESET button to restore the initial configuration. Newton invented a formula for calculating the force of attraction between two bodies. He used it to calculate the force needed to keep the moon moving around the earth. Then he compared it with the force that made the apple fall downward. After allowing for the fact that the moon is much farther from the earth, and has a much greater mass, he discovered that the forces were the same. The moon is held in an orbit around the earth by the pull of earth's gravity. I put power in my bike too. Teaching Work: Resources for Labor Day Declared an official national holiday in 1894, the roots of Labor Day stretch back much further. Long before the Industrial Revolution and the rise of organized labor, colonists and Native peoples labored to provide for themselves and their families and to support their societies. U.S. citizens continue to work in many ways and at many jobs. How has work changed over time? Take a look at our Labor Day spotlight page. We've gathered lesson plans, website reviews, teaching strategies, and more on American labor history. Using these resources, you and your students can ask questions about the nature of work and search for answers. Need some questions to get started? For each particular time and place, ask: - Was work divided by gender? What tasks were associated with each gender? How strictly were these divisions followed? How have they changed over time? - Similarly, was work divided by age? What tasks were associated with different ages? How strictly were these divisions followed? How have they changed over time? - What did people consider "work"? What did the word mean? Were work and play distinct? - How were differing ideas of work addressed or resolved? Think about contact between Native peoples and colonists, or between groups of immigrants from different countries. - Why do people work? How were they compensated (if at all)? - How was work regulated? Did the people doing the work make the rules? - What skills, education, or background knowledge were required for various jobs? - Did people choose to do work? What might the consequences be if they did not work? There are many more questions to ask! Brainstorm with your students. Ask them what they think of as work. Do they like it? Why do they do it? What kinds of work do the adults in their lives do? They may be surprised at how much the idea of work has changed over time-and even how much it varies from person to person today. Usually, sex is determined by an organism's genes. With genetic sex determination, most alleles or genes that influence sexual development are on the same chromosome. That chromosome is then called the sex chromosome. Because genetic sex determination is controlled by a pair of sex chromosomes (or if of one of the chromosomes is there or not), there are usually the same number of male and female offspring. In humans, for instance, sperms carry either an X or a Y chromosome, and they occur in roughly equal numbers. What d' you think she does in the circus? Title: How to Breed a Pet Ferret Introduction: Ferrets are fascinating animals with unique behaviors and personalities. If you're considering breeding your pet ferrets, there are several factors to consider before proceeding. This process requires careful planning, knowledge about ferret genetics, and responsible husbandry practices. This guide will walk you through the steps involved in breeding pet ferrets while providing crucial information and tips along the way. Disclaimer: Before starting, ensure that both jills (female ferrets) and hobs (male ferrets) used for breeding are healthy and have been screened for genetic defects and diseases like adrenal gland disease and insulinoma. Additionally, always consult a veterinarian experienced with ferrets prior to beginning any breeding program. Note that some regions may require specific permits or licenses for breeding ferrets; check local regulations first. Step 1: Choosing Suitable Jills and Hobs To start, select healthy adult ferrets who display no signs of illness or behavioral issues. Aim for unrelated individuals as close relatives can increase the risk of producing offspring with congenital disorders. It is generally recommended to wait until the jill is at least one year old before her first litter due to potential complications during pregnancy and delivery if bred too young. Key Tips: - Research ferret bloodlines and ancestry to minimize risks associated with inbreeding. - Avoid breeding ferrets carrying known genetic defects or diseases. Step 2: Preparing the Jill for Mating She doesn't really have the patience for it. If you lose your other one it's nice to have this. He built a small house called a cocoon around himself. You can calculate this for any number of forces to find the total force just by adding up all the components. To understand this conceptually, see below: Imagine a ball being pushed by a force like we said above. Imagine that force as an arrow starting from the ball and extending outwards - its length will be dictated by the lengths of x and y for the force. So x = 10, y = 0 would be an arrow 5 units long, point east. With a little pythagoras, you could work out that an arrow representing x = 3, y = 4 would be 5 units long. Now imagine you can pick up all of these arrows and place them tip-to-tail. So the first arrow takes us east by 5, the second arrow's tail would be positioned at x = 5, y = 0 . The second arrow's tail is placed here. Its tip would then be at x = 8, y = 4 . If we had a third arrow to add, we would put its tail where the tip of arrow #2 is. Once you have laid all the arrows tip to tail, the final arrow's tip will be a new position. If you now draw an arrow such that it's tail starts at the tail of the ball/object (or simply at 0,0 if you're calculating total force), and its tip goes to the final arrow's tip, you will have a new arrow which represents the total force of all the other arrows. Eight n nine ten eleven twelve third... Being able to express measurements is a very practical skill that even advanced students of English may need practice doing. Are they comfortable stating fractions? Do they know that a decimal point is read as "point" as in ninety-eight point six (98.6)? How fluent are they in the U.S. system of measurements? Can they more or less understand weather forecasts using Fahrenheit? Here's a fun way to give your students some practice with the U.S. system of measurements. Feel free to share your own ways of practicing these skills. Set up five stations around the room. In pairs or small groups, have the students move from station to station performing each task. Make sure each student has a notebook and pencil. - Station 1 - Length. [Supplies needed = tape measure or yard stick] Task: Measure your partner(s). How tall are they? Record their heights in feet and inches. - Station 2- Weight. [Supplies needed = a scale and a medium sized trash bag] Task: Place all your shoes in the bag. How much do they weight? Record the weight in pounds (and ounces, if the scale is electronic). Alternative: Weigh someone's backpack or purse. - Station 3 - Liquid measurements. [Supplies needed = 3-4 empty milk or water bottles of different sizes] Task: Look at the labels. How much does the largest bottle hold? How much does the next largest bottle hold? How much does the smallest bottle hold? Record your answers using gallons, quarts, and pints. (If possible, also record how many fluid ounces there are in each container.) - Station 4 - Cooking measurements. [Supplies needed = bag of flour, measuring spoons, measuring cups, coffee mug] Task: How many cups of flour fit in the coffee mug? How many tablespoons are needed to fill one cup? How many teaspoons are needed to fill a tablespoon? - Station 5 - Temperature. [ Supplies needed = weather thermometer, calculator (optional)] Task: What's the temperature of the classroom in Fahrenheit? How much is that in Celsius? Note: F to C = Subtract 32, then multiply by 5, then divide by 9. Sara was a big sister. She had a baby brother named Tom. Sara loved Tom very much, but sometimes she was jealous. She wanted to play with Mom and Dad, but they were busy with Tom. One day, Sara saw Tom sleeping in his crib. She had an idea. She took her doctor kit and went to examine Tom. She put a stethoscope on his chest, a thermometer in his mouth, and a band-aid on his arm. She said, "I am a doctor. I will make you better, Tom." But Tom did not like Sara's game. He woke up and started to cry. He spit out the thermometer and pulled off the band-aid. He was scared and hurt. He wanted Mom and Dad. Mom and Dad heard Tom's cry and ran to his room. They saw Sara and her doctor kit. They were angry and worried. They said, "Sara, what did you do to Tom? You should not play with him like that. You could hurt him or make him sick. He is a baby. He needs love and care, not games and toys." Sara felt sorry and ashamed. She said, "I am sorry, Mom and Dad. I wanted to play with you, but you were busy with Tom. I wanted to examine him, like you do. I wanted to be intelligent, like you are." Mom and Dad hugged Sara and said, "Sara, we love you very much. You are a big sister and a smart girl. You can play with us and with Tom, but you have to be gentle and kind. You can examine him, but only with your eyes and your words. You can ask him questions, like how he feels, what he likes, and what he learns. You can help him grow and learn, too. That is how you show your intelligence and your love." Sara smiled and said, "Thank you, Mom and Dad. I understand. I will be a good sister and a good doctor. I will love Tom and play with him nicely." She went to Tom's crib and said, "Hi, Tom. I am sorry I scared you. I love you. Do you want to play with me?" Tom stopped crying and looked at Sara. He smiled and reached for her hand. He said, "Yes, Sara. I love you, too. You are my sister. You are intelligent." The end. Each barrier is 1 yd 2 ft long. How many barriers are needed? 100 yds = 300 ft; 1 yd 2 ft = 5 ft 100/5 = 20 barriers 4) Write a realistic problem situation for 2 tons 850 lbs - 1 ton 1430 lbs. " Since the first humans, hunting became a way of life. It was and still is in some places, the key to survival. Mankind is distinguished from other animals by the use of tools. At least 2 million years ago man learned to hunt animals for meat. They learned to create and use spears and axes. Bows and arrows were used at least 71,000 years ago. Modern hunting still uses more primitive tools. But many subsistence hunters have adapted to use modern firearms. In modern countries hunting is still allowed but is considered a sport. Hunting can be used to control the populations of many animal species. As with many aspects of gun ownership and use, modern hunting remains controversial. Handguns are not generally used for hunting. Frederick William II had Prussia join the war with France in 1792. He lost at Valmy and gave his western land to France. Title: How to Find the Third Angle of a Triangle A triangle is a three-sided polygon with interior angles that always add up to 180 degrees. If you know the measures of two angles in a triangle, you can find the measure of the third angle by subtracting the sum of the known angles from 180 degrees. This article will provide a detailed, step-by-step guide on how to find the third angle of a triangle using this principle. ### Method 1: Using the 180-Degree Sum Rule **Step 1:** Identify the given angles Look at your triangle and identify which angles are provided or have been previously determined. In physics, when you have a vector, you have to keep in mind two quantities: its direction and its magnitude. Quantities that have only a magnitude are called scalars. If you give a scalar magnitude a direction, you create a vector. Visually, you see vectors drawn as arrows, which is perfect because an arrow has both a clear direction and a clear magnitude (the length of the arrow). Take a look at the following figure. The arrow represents a vector that starts at the arrow's foot (also called the tail) and ends at the head. In physics, you generally use a letter in bold type to represent a vector, although you may also see a letter with an arrow on top like this: The arrow means that this is not only a scalar value, which would be represented by A, but also something with direction. Say that you tell some smartypants that you know all about vectors. When he asks you to give him a vector, A, you give him not only its magnitude but also its direction, because you need these two bits of info together to define this vector. That impresses him to no end! For example, you may say that A is a vector at 15 from the horizontal with a magnitude of 12 meters/second. Smartypants knows all he needs to know, including that A is a velocity vector. Take a look at this figure, which features two vectors, A and B. They look pretty much the same - the same length and the same direction. In fact, these vectors are equal. Two vectors are equal if they have the same magnitude and direction, and you can write this as A = B. Title: How to Get Free Cable Television Disclaimer: This article is intended to provide information only. We do not condone or encourage any illegal activities, including theft of cable television services. Always respect copyright laws and pay for the content you consume. Introduction: Cable television can be an expensive monthly bill, but there are ways to get access to it without paying. However, it's important to note that these methods may not always be legal or ethical. This guide will explore various techniques that people have used to obtain free cable TV service. Remember, if you choose to pursue any of these options, you do so at your own risk and responsibility. Step 1: Use Antenna Detection Tools Key Tip: Before attempting to tap into a cable line, first determine whether the signal coming into your house is already encrypted. You can purchase an antenna detection tool online or from electronics stores. These devices detect the presence of unencrypted cable signals and help ensure you aren't trying to steal an encrypted feed. Step 2: Locate Nearby Unsecured Cables Guideline: Scout around your neighborhood for exposed outdoor cables. Look near utility poles, construction sites, or other areas where maintenance work might have been done recently. Be cautious about climbing onto rooftops or entering private properties without permission. Step 3: Connecting to the Cable Warning: Tampering with utility equipment is dangerous and illegal! Proceed with extreme caution and understand the potential consequences. Connect a coaxial cable from the wall outlet in your home directly to the unsecured cable you found outside. Make sure both ends are tightly screwed in place. If successful, you should now receive basic cable channels. Step 4: Install a Cable Splitter (Optional) If you want to share the connection with others or connect multiple televisions, install a cable splitter. Screw one end of the splitter onto the connected cable, then attach another coaxial cable from the splitter to each additional device. Step 5: Encryption Bypass (Advanced Users Only) Some cable companies scramble their signals using encryption technology. To bypass this, you'll need specialized hardware like a descrambler box or card reader. Please note that obtaining such equipment may involve questionable practices and could lead to legal repercussions. Step 6: Using IPTV Services Another method to get free cable involves utilizing Internet Protocol Television (IPTV) services. IPTV allows users to stream live TV over the internet. While some IPTV providers require payment, many offer free content as well. Keep in mind though, much of the 'free' content available through IPTV consists of pirated material which infringes upon copyright law. Conclusion: While getting free cable tv sounds appealing, remember that most of these methods violate terms of service agreements and local laws. It's best to stick to legitimate means of watching television, such as subscribing to a streaming service or purchasing a digital antenna for over-the-air broadcasts. Not only does this keep you within the bounds of legality, but it also supports the creators who produce the shows and movies we enjoy. They are seen as "universal," which means they are meant for everyone, no matter what their race, religion, ethnicity, nationality, age, sex, political beliefs (or any other kind of beliefs), intelligence, disability, sexual orientation, or gender identity are. You can list the ideas in a chart OR I like to make sentence strips of different colors for each sentence "part" for the kids to manipulate and make into one long "juicy" sentence. They have to use one strip of each color for their sentence (1 adjective, 1 noun/subject, 1 verb, etc). Then, I ask the kids to draw a picture to go with their writing. The picture has to show all the things that are going on in their sentence. One idea I had was to use these inexpensive writing journals from Starfall.com for this purpose. They have space for a drawing at the top of the page and space to write at the bottom. Saves on copy paper and keeps all their writing together! Cleaning a laptop keyboard effectively is an essential task that can help maintain your device's appearance, hygiene, and performance. Over time, dust, crumbs, oils, and other debris can accumulate between the keys, leading to reduced functionality or even damage. This comprehensive guide will provide you with detailed steps, helpful tips, and crucial guidelines to clean your laptop keyboard thoroughly without causing any harm. **Things You'll Need:** 1. A microfiber cloth or lint-free cloth 2. Isopropyl alcohol (70% or higher) or distilled water 3. Cotton swabs or small cleaning brushes 4. Compressed air duster (optional) 5. Disinfectant wipes (optional) ## **Step 1: Turn Off Your Laptop and Unplug It** Before starting the cleaning process, ensure your safety by turning off your laptop and unplugging it from the power source. Additionally, disconnect any peripherals such as USB drives or external mice. This step prevents accidental activation of keys while cleaning and reduces the risk of electrical shock. ## **Step 2: Use a Microfiber Cloth to Dust the Keyboard** Take a dry microfiber cloth or lint-free cloth and gently wipe over the entire keyboard surface. This action removes loose particles, dirt, and smudges. Avoid using paper towels or abrasive materials, which may scratch the delicate surfaces of your keyboard. ## **Step 3: Prepare a Cleaning Solution** Mix equal parts of 70% or higher isopropyl alcohol and distilled water in a small container. Alternatively, you can use only distilled water if you don't have access to rubbing alcohol. The solution should be damp but not soaking wet. Using a cleaning solution instead of plain water ensures better germ removal and quicker evaporation. ## **Step 4: Dampen a Cotton Swab or Brush** Dip a cotton swab or a small brush into the prepared cleaning solution. Gently squeeze out excess liquid to prevent moisture from seeping beneath the keys. If you choose to use a brush, make sure it has soft bristles to avoid damaging the keyboard. ## **Step 5: Clean Between the Keys** Use the dampened cotton swab or brush to carefully scrub between individual keys. Pay particular attention to areas where debris tends to collect, like around the edges of keys or near the spacebar. Be gentle during this process; excessive force might cause liquid to enter the internal components of your laptop. ## **Step 6: Dry the Keyboard Thoroughly** After cleaning between the keys, allow them to air-dry completely before turning on your laptop. To expedite the drying process, you can use a hairdryer set at its lowest heat setting or point a fan towards the keyboard. Make sure all traces of moisture are gone before proceeding. ### *Key Tips and Guidelines:* * Never pour liquids directly onto your keyboard, as this could lead to irreversible damage. * Don't eat or drink near your laptop to minimize the chances of food particles falling into the keyboard. * Regularly blow compressed air into the keyboard to remove debris and prolong the need for deeper cleans. * For stubborn stains, consider using specialized cleaning products designed explicitly for laptops. Always follow manufacturer instructions when using these products. * Periodically disinfect your keyboard using disinfectant wipes or cloths. Remember to let it dry entirely after disinfection. Once upon a time, there was a boy named Timmy. Timmy had a toy pipe that he liked to play with. The pipe was very popular with all of his friends, and they all wanted to play with it too. One day, Timmy and his friends were playing at the park when they saw two birds fighting in the sky. They all looked up and saw the birds flying around and making lots of noise. Timmy thought it looked like fun, so he pretended to fight with his friends too. After they finished playing, Timmy went home and showed his mom the pipe. She thought it was a nice toy, but reminded him to be careful with it. Timmy promised to take good care of it and put it away in his toy box. Title: How to Care for Your Doll's Hair Introduction: Doll hair, like human hair, requires proper care and maintenance to keep it looking its best. Whether you have a vintage porcelain doll or a modern vinyl one, this guide will provide you with comprehensive steps on how to clean, brush, restyle, and store your doll's hair. With patience and consistency, these methods can help extend the lifespan of your doll's hairstyle and overall appearance. Cleaning Your Doll's Hair: 1. Gather Necessary Materials: Before starting the cleaning process, make sure you have gathered all necessary materials. This includes a mild shampoo (such as baby shampoo), a bowl of warm water, a soft-bristle toothbrush, paper towels, and a clean cloth or microfiber towel. Avoid using harsh chemicals or hot water, which may damage the hair fibers. 2. Prepare the Shampoo Solution: Mix a few drops of shampoo into the bowl of warm water. Stir until suds form but avoid creating too many bubbles, as they might be difficult to rinse out completely. 3. Submerge the Hair: Slowly submerge the doll's hair into the soapy solution. Make sure every strand is wet, but do not saturate the head beyond what's needed. Be cautious when handling delicate dolls to prevent any breakage or damage. 4. Gentle Lathering: Using the soft-bristle toothbrush, gently work up a lather within the dampened hair. Focus on areas that appear dirty or oily. Remember, there's no need to scrub vigorously; just enough pressure should be applied to remove dirt and grime without causing harm. 5. Rinsing Process: After lathering, carefully rinse the soap from the hair under running lukewarm water. To ensure thorough rinsing, consider flipping the doll's head upside down during this step. Again, avoid using hot water to prevent heat damage. 6. Blot Dry: Lay the doll flat on several layers of paper towels. Use another dry paper towel to blot excess moisture from the hair. Never rub, twist, or wring the hair as doing so could cause tangling or breakage. 7. Air Dry Completely: Allow the doll's hair to air dry naturally away from direct sunlight or heat sources. Speed up the drying process by intermittently patting the hair with a clean, dry cloth or microfiber towel. It's crucial to let the hair dry entirely before proceeding to brushing or styling to minimize risk of damage. Brushing Your Doll's Hair: 8. Choose an Appropriate Brush: Select a high-quality brush specifically designed for doll hair. Bristles made of boar hair or nylon are generally safe choices due to their gentle nature. For more intricate styles or textured hair, specialized brushes may yield better results. 9. Divide the Hair: Section off small portions of the hair to make detangling easier. Hold each section firmly yet gently near the root to prevent pulling or tearing. Beginning at the ends, slowly brush through knots and tangles moving upward toward the scalp. 10. Remove Excess Moisture: If the hair still feels slightly damp after air drying, lightly run the brush over the surface to eliminate remaining moisture. This step makes combing smoother and reduces static electricity buildup. Restyling Your Doll's Hair: It's taking pictures of you while you're doing all of this. [finishing SAA's hairband has caught some of SAA's shorter front strands in hairband, so braid sticks up in front of SAA's face.] Pressure is the measure of how much pushing force something is putting on another object. In a gas, this is usually the gas pushing on the container of the object or, if the gas is heavy, something inside the gas. Pressure is measured in pascals. Because of Newton's third law, we can change the pressure of a gas by putting force on the object containing it. For example, squeezing a bottle with air inside pressurises (gives more pressure) to the air inside. Where does this guy [silver man] go? Video by Lynn Brunelle Tip of the Iceberg Icebergs are huge floating islands of ice that float in the ocean. When you see an iceberg you are only seeing about 10% of the whole thing. The rest of the iceberg is floating below the surface of the water. How is that possible? Water is weird. Unlike most liquids, it expands when it freezes. Because of this expansion, ice floats in water. You can see the expansion with this experiment. What you need A plastic pop bottle A sharpie pen A freezer What you do 1. Fill the pop bottle about full of tap water and mark the level on the outside of the bottle with the pen. 2. Stick it in the freezer and let it sit for a few hours. 3. Pull out the bottle. Check out where the level of the ice is in comparison to where the water was. What's going on? To do & notice The ice expanded when it froze. Most liquids shrink when they freeze. As the temperature drops, the molecules slow down and pull tighter together. This makes the substance more dense. Denser objects sink in less dense fluids. Solid lead sinks in liquid lead and solid steel sinks in liquid steel. If this were true of ice and water, ice would be denser than water and would sink rather than float. But it doesn't. With water, the solid form, ice, is less dense than the liquid form, water. So it floats. How can this be? As water is cooled, it does become denser and denser, but only until it reaches a temperature just before freezing. At this point, the molecules line up with spaces in between. It expands to form crystals that are in fact less dense than liquid water. So ice floats. Oil and Ice Oil and water don't mix. Oil will float on top of water. It's less dense. But what about oil and ice? What Do You Need? A 2 liter pop bottle with the top part removed Cooking oil An ice cube What Do You Do? Fill the bottle about half full with cooking oil. Drop the ice cube in, and watch what happens as the cube melts. What's going on? An ice cube looks more solid than oil, but its molecules are a lot less densely packed together. When the ice melts and turns to liquid water, the molecules become denser. You can see that happen when the melted drops fall in perfect little spheres down through the oil, coming to rest at the bottom of the bottle. Want to Get Technical? The next time your friends or parents have iced water, you could give a fancy explanation for floating ice. Casually mention that ice floats because it has a density of 0.8 grams per cubic centimeter while water has a greater density of 1 gram per cubic centimeter. They'll be amazed! Since the ovules are protected by carpels, it takes something special for fertilisation to happen. Angiosperms have pollen grains comprising just three cells. One cell is responsible for drilling down through the integuments, and creating a passage for the two sperm cells to flow down. The megagametophyte is a tiny haploid female plant which includes the egg. It has just seven cells. Of these, one is the egg cell; it fuses with a sperm cell, forming the zygote. Another cell joins with the other sperm, and dedicates itself to forming a nutrient-rich endosperm. The other cells take auxiliary roles. This process of "double fertilisation" is unique and common to all angiosperms. Hi there! Today we're going to talk about something called "linear programming," which is just a fancy name for finding the best solution to a problem using math. It's kind of like solving a puzzle, but instead of using shapes or pictures, we use numbers and equations. Let me give you an example. Imagine you have a lemonade stand and you want to make as much money as possible. You know that you need lemons, sugar, and water to make your lemonade. Each lemon costs $0.50, each cup of sugar costs $1.00, and each gallon of water costs $0.25. But you only have a certain amount of money to spend on ingredients. So, how do you figure out how many lemons, cups of sugar, and gallons of water to buy to make the most money? This is where linear programming comes in. We can set up an equation to represent our problem. Let's say L represents the number of lemons, S represents the number of cups of sugar, W represents the number of gallons of water, and M represents the total amount of money we make selling the lemonade. Our equation would look something like this: M = $0.50L + $1.00S + $0.25W But wait, we also have some restrictions. We can only spend a certain amount of money on ingredients. Let's say we have $10.00 to spend. That means we need another equation to represent our spending limit: $0.50L + $1.00S + $0.25W <= $10.00 Now we have two equations that describe our problem. The first equation tells us how much money we make based on the number of lemons, cups of sugar, and gallons of water we use. The second equation makes sure we don't spend more than $10.00 on ingredients. Using linear programming, we can find the values of L, S, and W that will give us the maximum value of M while still satisfying our spending limit. This is done by using algorithms, which are basically step-by-step instructions for solving problems. There are different ways to solve linear programming problems, including something called the "simplex method." However, this method can be tricky because it relies on making decisions in a specific order. Sometimes it helps to work on multiple parts of the problem at once, which is why people developed other methods, such as "Khachiyan's method" and "Karmarkar's method." These methods allow us to solve linear programming problems faster and more efficiently. In summary, linear programming is a way to find the best solution to a problem using math. By setting up equations with limitations, we can use algorithms to determine the optimal values for our variables. Even though it may sound complicated, it's actually similar to solving puzzles, and can be applied to real-world situations like running a lemonade stand! Title: How to Cancel Huddle App Subscription on Your iPhone or iPad Introduction: Huddle is a popular app used for collaboration and project management among teams. However, there might come a time when you no longer need its services, and want to cancel your subscription. This tutorial will guide you through the process of cancelling your Huddle app subscription using your iPhone or iPad. Step 1: Open Settings App On the home screen of your device, locate the gray gear icon labeled 'Settings'. Tap on this icon to open the settings application where you manage various system preferences. Key Tip: If you are unsure about any setting or option while following these steps, simply tap and hold the text until the 'Look Up' option appears; then release the text and select 'Look Up', which provides definitions and additional information from Apple's built-in dictionary. Step 2: Access Your Apple ID Profile Scroll down to find the section with your name at the top. It should be located just above the options like iTunes & App Store, Photos, Mail, etc. Tapping on your name takes you to your Apple ID profile page. Guideline: Make sure that you have signed into your correct Apple account as sometimes users may have multiple accounts linked to their devices. You don't want to end up cancelling someone else's subscription accidentally! Step 3: Manage Subscriptions From your Apple ID profile page, scroll down until you see the heading 'Subscriptions'. Underneath this header, tap on the 'Manage' button to view all active subscriptions associated with your Apple ID. Step 4: Locate Huddle App Subscription Once redirected to the 'Subscriptions Management' page, look for Huddle within the list of active subscriptions. The apps are listed alphabetically, making them easy to locate. After finding Huddle, tap on it to proceed further. Step 5: Cancel Subscription After selecting Huddle, you'll arrive at its individual subscription management page. Here, under the 'Active' tab, you'll notice an 'Expires' date along with other details regarding your current plan. To stop automatic renewal, toggle off the switch next to 'Renew Automatically'. A confirmation alert message will pop up saying 'Turning Off Auto-Renewal...'; confirm by tapping 'Confirm'. Key Tips: * Remember, disabling auto-renewal does NOT refund you for unused portions of your existing billing cycle. Instead, it means you won't be billed again after your current period ends. * Be aware of the expiration date so that you aren't surprised if features locked behind the paywall return during the wind-down phase. Step 6: Verify Cancellation To ensure successful cancellation, go back to the 'Subscriptions Management' page (refer Step 3). Check whether the status of the Huddle app has changed to 'Expired' instead of 'Active'. Also, verify the 'Next Billing Date' shows as N/A or None. Conclusion: Congratulations! By completing these six steps, you have successfully cancelled your Huddle app subscription on your iPhone or iPad. Now sit back, relax, and enjoy the freedom of not being charged anymore. Don't forget to delete the app itself if you wish to do so now that it serves no purpose without an active subscription. Why does Gabor have a bad tooth? Title: How to Draw a Perfect Rectangle Drawing a rectangle may seem like a simple task, but achieving precision and accuracy can be challenging. This step-by-step guide will provide you with detailed instructions on drawing a perfect rectangle, along with useful tips and guidelines. **Step 1: Understand the Basics** A rectangle is a four-sided polygon with opposite sides being equal and parallel. It has four right angles (90 degrees). Familiarizing yourself with these basic properties will help you draw an accurate shape. **Step 2: Choose Your Medium** Decide whether you want to draw your rectangle using traditional tools such as pencil and paper, digital software, or online platforms. Each medium has its advantages; for instance, traditional methods allow more control over lines while digital ones offer easy undos and symmetry options. **Step 3: Determine the Size** Before starting to draw, decide on the size of your rectangle. You can do this by mentally noting dimensions or physically measuring them out if you're working traditionally. If digitally drawing, most programs have ruler tools that let you set precise measurements. **Step 4: Mark Two Points** Identify two points where one pair of opposite corners will be located. These should be far enough apart to accommodate your chosen size. Accuracy here ensures the rest of the process goes smoothly. **Step 5: Draw Horizontal Lines** Connect the initial two points with both horizontal and vertical lines. To ensure straightness, use a ruler or guideline feature in digital softwares. The length between these two lines represents the height of your rectangle. **Step 6: Draw Vertical Lines** Now connect the top and bottom edges at their ends with two perpendicular lines - again ensuring they are perfectly vertical. Remember, all internal angles within a rectangle must be right angles (90 ). Use your protractor or check against gridlines in digital softwares to confirm. **Key Tip:** *To make sure your rectangles are symmetrical, consider folding your paper along either the horizontal or vertical axis after Step 5. Any discrepancies will show up as creases when unfolded.* **Step 7: Check Proportions** Double-check that the lengths and widths match your intended design from Step 3. Small errors can add up quickly, so catching mistakes now saves time later. **Step 8: Refine Edges** Go over your lines gently to neaten any rough patches or uneven line thicknesses. Thin consistent lines typically look cleanest. **Guidelines:** 1. Practice regularly to improve speed and accuracy. 2. Consider gridding your page if drawing freehand to maintain proportions. 3. Utilize technology like smart guides in graphic design applications for automated alignment assistance. 4. Learn basic perspective principles for creating three-dimensional looking rectangles. By following these steps meticulously, you'll soon master the art of drawing a perfect rectangle every time! It's a book so each letter pasta be capitalized. and I are ready to go down. Take a text and cut it into small phrase-sized or sentence-sized chunks. Divide the class into groups - groups of 4-5 works best. Keep the cut up texts at the front of the class, numbered and one for each team. Give each group the first part of the text, and they need to translate it and bring it back to you for approval. When you're satisfied, you can give the group the next part of the text. Repeat until done! This activity has the potential to become quite chaotic, so here are a few things I have learned from running it! - Colour-code the texts somehow. You could print out the texts in different colours or just scribble a line along the top with a coloured pen/pencil. - Keep all of the texts at the front. I pre-cut them so they can be like tear-off strips, and tape them to a desk so students can tear them off - put these on a desk but not the one where you're marking as this will cause a traffic jam! - Stay rooted to the front and enforce a queue! You may end up with 2-3 students queuing but because the phrases are short they are quickly marked. - Don't correct mistakes, just highlight where they are. (This makes the students REALLY work hard on accuracy!) - Watch the time! It's very busy for the teacher as well as for the students and it's easy to get carried away. This activity could easily take a whole lesson if you want it to. - Use some kind of scoreboard so the groups can see where the others are up to. I use a single page on the whiteboard and let students tick off each number, or But no one is born knowing how to set goals and develop a plan to carry them out. Reader Jane Haney suggests five steps to help your child learn how to set goals . . . and achieve them: - At the beginning of the week, help your child identify one challenging but attainable goal. It might be turning in a book report on time. It might be getting 90% correct on a spelling test. - Have your child write the goal on a piece of paper. Post it on the refrigerator or a bulletin board. - Talk about how to accomplish the goal. Help your child break the goal down into smaller steps. For example, "You could read two chapters every day. Then you can spend a day writing your report and another day revising it." - As the week progresses, ask how things are going. If problems come up, talk about possible solutions. If your child falls behind in reading, for example, a ten-minute extension of bedtime might encourage him to catch up. - At the end of the week, help your child evaluate how well she did. Did she achieve her goal? Why or why not? Most important, praise your child for trying. Then set a new goal for next week. Copyright Parent Institute What do you do with food Scarlett?
