Developing an online shopping cart is a complex process that involves several steps and requires knowledge in various areas such as web development, database management, and user experience design. Here's a step-by-step guide to help you through this process: **Step 1: Define Your Business Requirements** Before starting any project, it's crucial to define what you want to achieve with your online shopping cart. Identify the types of products or services you will sell, payment methods, shipping options, tax calculations, etc. This will give you a clear picture of the functionality needed for your shopping cart. Key Tip: Always keep your target audience in mind when defining these requirements. The more user-friendly your shopping cart is, the better the customer experience, leading to higher sales conversion rates. **Step 2: Choose the Right E-commerce Platform** There are many e-commerce platforms available today like Magento, Shopify, WooCommerce (for WordPress), BigCommerce, etc. Each platform has its own strengths and weaknesses, so research thoroughly before making a decision based on factors such as ease of use, scalability, customizability, cost, and community support. Guideline: If you're not tech-savvy, consider using hosted solutions like Shopify or BigCommerce which offer easy setup processes and user-friendly interfaces. For greater control over design and functionality, self-hosted platforms like Magento or WooCommerce might be more suitable but may require advanced technical skills. **Step 3: Design Your Storefront** Your storefront should reflect your brand identity and provide a seamless browsing experience for customers. Use high-quality images, clear product descriptions, easy navigation menus, and search functions. Make sure your layout is responsive, meaning it adjusts according to different screen sizes for optimal viewing on desktops, tablets, and smartphones. Key Tip: Keep loading times minimal by optimizing images and limiting excessive scripts or plugins. A slow-loading site can deter potential customers and negatively impact SEO rankings. **Step 4: Set Up Product Listings** Add all your products along with relevant details such as name, description, price, SKU, weight, dimensions, stock status, categories, tags, etc. Also, decide whether you want to allow customer reviews and ratings. Guideline: Organize your catalog carefully into categories and subcategories. Provide filters and sorting options to make finding specific items easier for shoppers. Title: How to Perform a Manual on a Tech Deck A manual is a trick often performed by skateboarders where they balance the board on two wheels while rolling. This guide will teach you how to do a manual on a Tech Deck, which is a fingerboard that simulates the experience of riding a real skateboard but at a smaller scale. With patience and practice, even beginners can learn this impressive-looking move. **Things You'll Need:** * A Tech Deck or any other brand of fingerboard with trucks and wheels * Smooth surface (e.g., table, desk) * Fingertips without cuts or injuries **Step 1: Understanding the Basics** The first thing to understand about doing manuals on a Tech Deck is that it requires balancing the board on its back truck while moving forward or backward. The front truck lifts off the ground as you lean back slightly, creating an illusion of weightlessness. It may take several tries before mastering this technique due to its inherent instability. **Key Tips:** * Practice good finger placement - Place your index fingers near the middle of the deck, just above the trucks, ensuring control over both ends of the board. * Maintain pressure - Keep consistent downward force using your fingertips throughout the entire process. **Step 2: Getting Comfortable with Your Tech Deck** Before attempting a manual, get comfortable with simply pushing and steering your Tech Deck around a flat surface. Make sure you have full command of the movement and direction of the board. **Key Guidelines:** * Start slow - Begin with gentle pushes and gradually increase speed once confident. * Steer smoothly - Use small adjustments to change directions rather than abrupt movements. **Step 3: Learning the Takeoff** Start practicing lifting one end of the board slightly so only two wheels are touching the ground. Focus on keeping the raised end close to the other wheelset without tipping over. **Key Tips:** * Gradually lift - Instead of jerking upwards, gently raise the nose of the board. * Find the sweet spot - Experiment with different positions until finding the right balance point. **Step 4: Mastering Balance** Once you can consistently lift the nose, work towards maintaining balance during motion. Try rocking back and forth between four wheels and two, getting a feel for how much weight shift is needed. **Key Tips:** * Move slowly - Initially, focus more on stability than speed. * Anticipate transitions - As you roll along, prepare yourself mentally for when to switch from four to two wheels. **Step 5: Performing the Manual** Now that you've practiced all necessary components, combine them into performing a complete manual. Push off, transition onto two wheels, maintain balance, then return to four wheels. Remember, keep applying steady pressure through your fingers. **Key Guidelines:** * Momentum matters - Gaining some momentum makes balancing easier. * Stay loose - Avoid tensing up; staying relaxed allows for smoother corrections. **Troubleshooting Common Problems:** * If the board keeps flipping over, check your finger positioning. Placing them too far apart could cause imbalance. * If you struggle to stay on two wheels, ensure you're shifting enough weight backwards. Remember, mastering tricks like these takes time and persistence. Don't be discouraged if progress seems slow initially. Happy shredding! Maybe you can tell Rachel what you're doing. However, it's important to consider the consequences of deactivation before proceeding. Once you deactivate your Reddit account, all of your posts, comments, and other contributions will no longer be visible to other users. Additionally, you will lose access to any private messages or communities you were a part of. Here are the steps to deactivate your Reddit account: 1. Log in to your Reddit account. Before you can deactivate your account, you must first log in using your username and password. This ensures that only the true owner of the account can deactivate it. 2. Navigate to the Preferences page. To do this, click on your user icon (located at the top right corner of the screen) and select "User Settings" from the dropdown menu. Then, click on the "Preferences" tab located at the left side of the screen. 3. Scroll down to the bottom of the Preferences page. Here, you will find the "Deactivate Account" section. 4. Read the warning message carefully. Before you can deactivate your account, Reddit requires you to read and acknowledge a warning message. This message informs you that once you deactivate your account, all of your data will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered. If you understand the implications of deactivation and still wish to proceed, check the box next to the statement "I understand that my account and its contents will be unrecoverable." 5. Enter your password. Next, you will need to enter your password into the provided field. This serves as an additional security measure to prevent unauthorized individuals from deactivating your account. 6. Click the "Deactivate" button. After entering your password, click the red "Deactivate" button to initiate the deactivation process. A confirmation message will appear, asking if you are sure you want to deactivate your account. If you are certain, click the "Deactivate" button again to finalize the process. 7. Wait for the deactivation to complete. The deactivation process may take several minutes to complete. During this time, do not attempt to refresh the page or navigate away from it. Once the deactivation is complete, you will be automatically logged out of your account and redirected to the Reddit homepage. Key Tips and Guidelines: * It's important to note that deactivating your Reddit account does not necessarily delete it entirely. While your content will no longer be visible to other users, your account information may still be stored by Reddit for legal or administrative purposes. * If you simply want to take a break from Reddit without completely deleting your account, consider setting your profile to "private" instead. This will hide your activity from other users while preserving your content and community memberships. * If you have multiple Reddit accounts, make sure to deactivate them individually. Deactivating one account will not affect any others associated with the same email address. * If you change your mind after deactivating your account, it may be possible to reactivate it within 30 days. To do so, simply log back in using your original username and password. However, keep in mind that some data may be lost during the deactivation process, such as personal messages or post history. By following these steps and guidelines, you can successfully deactivate your Reddit account and remove yourself from the platform. Remember to carefully consider your decision before proceeding, as the effects of deactivation are permanent and irreversible. Do you know what we have for dessert? [finds the ball on his father's desk] Title: How to Convert Square Meters to Square Feet Converting square meters to square feet is a simple process that involves multiplying or dividing numbers according to specific conversion factors. This skill can be helpful when dealing with architectural plans, real estate listings, interior design, landscaping projects, and other situations where area measurements are expressed in both metric (square meters) and imperial units (square feet). By following this detailed step-by-step guide, you will learn how to convert square meters to square feet accurately and confidently. ### Key Tips and Guidelines: 1. Understand the basics: One square meter is equal to 10.764 square feet. This means that if you want to convert square meters to square feet, you need to multiply the number of square meters by 10.764. Conversely, to convert square feet to square meters, divide the number of square feet by 10.764. 2. Be aware of significant figures and rounding errors: Depending on the context and required precision, keep track of significant figures during calculations and consider rounding your final answer accordingly. 3. Double-check your work: To ensure accuracy, always double-check your calculations using a reliable online converter or calculator as a reference. **Step 1:** Identify the number of square meters you wish to convert to square feet For example, let's say you have a room that measures 15 square meters, and you would like to find out its size in square feet. Written as an equation, this would look like: (15 sq m) x conversion factor = (?) sq ft **Step 2:** Multiply the number of square meters by the conversion factor (10.764) Multiplying 15 square meters by 10.764 gives us approximately 161.46 sq ft. So, our equation now looks like this: (15 sq m) x 10.764 = 161.46 sq ft This means that the room measuring 15 square meters has an equivalent area of about 161.46 square feet. **Step 3 (Optional):** Round your answer based on significant figures and context Depending on the situation, you may need to round your answer to a certain number of decimal places or significant figures. For instance, if this measurement was for residential purposes, you might round up to the nearest whole number since people typically don't talk about fractions of a square foot in everyday conversation. However, if you were working on a technical drawing or engineering project, you might choose to maintain more decimal points to preserve accuracy. In our example, we could round up to the nearest whole number (162 sq ft), given that there are no further digits beyond the '2'. Alternatively, if you wanted to express this value with one additional decimal place, you would write it as 161.5 sq ft. By carefully following these steps and considering the relevant tips and guidelines provided, you should now feel equipped to tackle any square meter-to-square foot conversions that come your way! English in Use/Time and Date |General||Contents Introduction| |Parts of speech||Articles Nouns Verbs Gerunds and participles Pronouns Adjectives Adverbs Prepositions, Conjunctions and Interjections| |Other topics||Orthography Punctuation Syntax Figures of Syntax Glossary| Here are some hints on how to express the date and time in English: - The day of the month, the month, and the year: - Use on + ordinal of the day. On the 1st of June. - Use in + only and the month. In June. - Use about the + date (for approximate dates). About the 25th of December. - Day of the week: precedes on. Example: open on Sundays. - Ask the date: what's the date and what day is it (or today)?. - Answer a date asking: today's the + ordinal. Today's the 16th. - Century: in the + the ordinal of the century. Example: in the 20th century. - Precedes in with the number in plural: in the 1990s. - Follows something for undetermined year of the decade, in other words: in 1980 something. - A.D. = Anno Domini, Latin for in the year of the Lord, in other words the Christian Era. - B.C. = Before Christ, in other words before Christian Era. Last modified on 24 June 2010, at 02:58 Jump back a section - Ask the time: What time is it? and What is the time? - Answer: It is and the part of the hour or the minutes, seconds: It is five o'clock. - Parts of the day: - Parts of the hour: - O'clock, for example: It is one o'clock (1:00). - Past and to: - It is 4 minutes past 5 (5:04) (or 4 minutes after 5 in USA). - It is 20 to 5 (4:40) (or 20 before 5 in USA). - A quarter past, for example: It is a quarter past 9 (9:15) (or a quarter after 9 in USA). - A quarter to, for example: It is a quarter to 12 (11:45) (or a quarter before 12 in USA). - Half past, for example: It is half past 9 (9:30). - Answer to when?: - Certain time: At, for example: At 5 p.m.. - Nearly, about : It is about 10 o'clock. - Just after: It is just after 10 (a few minutes past 10). - Synonyms for times: - Noon, Midday: 12:00 in the 24 hour clock. It is 5 past noon (12:05). - Midnight: 0:00 in the 24 hour clock. Half past midnight (00:30). Will it hurt asked sister not nearly as much as biting your tongue or bumping your shin said the doctor. Title: Understanding Light and How It Behaves in Different Materials Have you ever wondered why a straw in a glass of water or juice looks bent or broken? That happens because of something called refraction - it's when light changes direction as it passes through different materials like water, juice, or even ice! Today, let's learn about this interesting phenomenon using a simple example with light traveling through ice. Imagine you are outside on a sunny day, holding a stopwatch and a long stick. You place one end of the stick into a thick block of solid ice. Now, ask a friend to stand near you and shine a flashlight towards the other end of the stick inside the ice block. When the light enters the ice, you start your stopwatch, and when the light comes out of the ice and reaches you again, you stop the timer. The time it takes for the light to travel through the ice tells us its speed in that material. Let's say the light took 0.45 seconds to go through the ice and back. Since you know that the distance between you and your friend is 45 meters (the length of the ray going into and coming out of the ice), you can calculate the speed of light in ice by dividing the total distance traveled by the time taken: Speed of light in ice = Distance / Time = 45 meters / 0.9 seconds 2.29 * 10^8 m/s Now, here's where things get really cool! Have you ever heard that light travels faster in a vacuum than any other material? This means that the speed of light in ice should be slower than its maximum possible speed, which is approximately 3 * 10^8 m/s, also known as the speed of light in a vacuum, c. To understand how much slower light moves in ice compared to a vacuum, scientists introduced something called the Refractive Index. The Refractive Index shows us the ratio of the speed of light in a vacuum ($c$) to the speed of light in another substance (in our case, ice). By calculating this ratio, we find out that the Refractive Index of ice is around 1.31: Refractive Index of ice = Speed of light in a vacuum / Speed of light in ice = 3 * 10^8 m/s / 2.29 * 10^8 m/s 1.31 So, now you know that when light travels from a vacuum into ice, it slows down to roughly 76% of its top speed due to the material properties of ice! And this difference in speeds results in the bending or refraction of light that you see when looking at a straw submerged in a drink! Pretty neat, huh? Sheep were grazed in the Berkshire countryside, particularly on the Berkshire Downs and the fields of the central part of the county. The 'Berkshire Knot Wether' was a popular breed of sheep. When the sheep had a nice thick fleece of wool, it was cut off using shears (like a big pair of scissors). Packmen would take the fleeces out to people in their cottages. They were carried on The women would brush the wool from the fleece with cards to make the fibres all go in the same direction. Cards were like big flat hair-brushes with teeth made from teasels. The women would then spin the wool into long threads called yarn. They used a spinning wheel to do this. The men would use a big wooden framed piece of equipment called a loom to weave the yarn togther. It crossed all the threads over one another very tightly to form cloth. The packmen would then take the cloth to the Fulling Mill. This was a water mill where the paddles of the water-wheel were used to beat the cloth and scrub it with 'fuller's earth' (known today as Aluminium Oxide). This made the cloth shrink and become thicker. There were fulling mills at Beenham, Thatcham, Brightwell, Seacourt and elsewhere. The cloth would then be taken to a man who would dip it in dye to make it change colour. Lots of natural dyes were used, using local After the cloth was dried, it was cut into lengths and sold (often in Belgium) by the Cloth Merchants. They controlled the whole process and got very Famous Berkshire Cloth Merchants include Jack of Newbury and John Kendrick (of Reading). Once there was a garden. It was so beautiful that the guard just watched in awe. He was very proud that he had made such a lovely garden. The guard continued to spray the garden every day, taking extra time to make the flowers look even more beautiful. Soon more and more animals came to the garden. Rabbits, birds and butterflies all fluttered around, taking in the beauty of the garden. The guard smiled, he was so proud of his work. Prevent the Flu |[ Video: Questions & Concerns: The Flu & The Flu Shot (Read video script) ] Influenza or "the flu" is an illness that affects our entire body, including the lungs. As many as 1 in 5 Americans get the flu each year. A virus that spreads from person to person causes flu. The illness can be mild, like a bad cold, or it can be very serious. In fact, people can die from the flu. You get the flu from other people. The flu virus spreads through coughing or sneezing. It is also spread when a person touches something that a sick person has touched, then touches their eyes, nose or mouth. Help Prevent the Flu - Get a flu vaccine each year. - Wash your hands often with soap and water. Wash them for as long as it takes to sing the Happy Birthday song twice. - Cover your nose and mouth with a tissue when you cough or sneeze, and then throw the tissue in the trash. - Stay home if you are sick until you have been symptom-free without taking fever reducing medicine for 24 hours. - Try to avoid close contact with sick people. - Eat a healthy diet, exercise and get plenty of rest. The dihydrogen monoxide parody involves calling water by the unfamiliar chemical name "dihydrogen monoxide" (DHMO) and listing some of its harmful effects in an alarming way. Some examples include talking about how "it causes burning, suffocation and corrosion," when it is actually just talking about hot water, drowning and rust. Sometimes the parody calls for it to be banned and/or labelled as dangerous. I don't know if it [the silver stick] will stay. I don't know what the story is from lasagna. It happened that Mr Bhosh was one day promenading down the Bayswater Road when he was passed by a white horse drawing a milk chariot with unparalleled velocity, outstripping omnibuses, waggons, and even butcher-carts in its wind-like progress, which was unguided by any restraining hand, for the milk-charioteer himself was pursuing on foot. His natural puissance in equine affairs enabled Mr Bhosh to infer that the steed which could cut such a record when handicapped with a cumbrous dairy chariot would exhibit even greater speed if in _puris naturalibus_, and that it might even not improbably carry off first prize in the Derby race. So, as the milk-charioteer ran up, overblown with anxiety, to learn the result of his horse's escapade, Mr Bhosh stopped him to inquire what he would take for such an animal. The dairy-vendor, rather foolishly taking it for granted that horse and cart were gone concerns, thought he was making the good stroke of business in offering the lot for a twenty-pound note. However, with careful planning and consideration, you can come up with a compelling and original research question that will form the basis of your dissertation. Here's a step-by-step guide on how to generate ideas for a dissertation topic: Step 1: Identify Your Interests and Passions Before you start generating ideas, it is essential to identify your interests and passions. Think about what topics excite you, and which areas you are most interested in studying further. This will help ensure that you stay motivated throughout the research process and produce high-quality work. Key tip: Reflect on previous coursework, internships, or work experience to help identify potential areas of interest. Step 2: Conduct Preliminary Research Once you have identified your interests, conduct preliminary research to gain a better understanding of the existing literature in those areas. Look for gaps in current knowledge or debates within the field that could provide inspiration for your dissertation topic. Key guideline: Use academic databases such as JSTOR, Google Scholar, or your university library catalog to search for relevant articles and books. Step 3: Brainstorm Possible Topics Based on your preliminary research, brainstorm possible dissertation topics. Try to think creatively and broadly at this stage - don't limit yourself to just one idea. Write down all potential topics, even if they seem far-fetched or unfeasible initially. Key tip: Set aside dedicated time for brainstorming sessions, and consider using techniques like mind mapping or free writing to stimulate creativity. Step 4: Assess Feasibility After brainstorming, assess the feasibility of each potential topic by considering factors such as available resources, data sources, and time constraints. Narrow down your list to two or three promising options. Key guideline: Consult with faculty advisors, librarians, or other experts to gauge whether your chosen topics are viable and well-suited for your skill level and expertise. Step 5: Formulate a Research Question For each remaining topic, craft a clear and concise research question that outlines the scope of your study. Make sure it is specific enough to guide your investigation but also leaves room for exploration and discovery. Key tip: Review successful dissertation abstracts in your field to get an idea of effective research questions. Step 6: Seek Feedback Share your draft research questions with colleagues, professors, or peers for feedback. They may offer valuable insights, suggestions, or criticisms that can help refine your focus and approach. Key guideline: Be open to constructive criticism and willing to make revisions based on others' input. Step 7: Finalize Your Topic Choose the final dissertation topic that best aligns with your interests, skills, and available resources. Confirm its viability with your faculty advisor before proceeding. Key tip: Remember that selecting a topic does not mean being locked into a rigid plan; allow for flexibility and evolution as you delve deeper into the subject matter. By following these steps, you will be able to generate a strong and well-considered dissertation topic that sets the foundation for a successful research project. Once upon a time, there was a little girl named Lily. One day, Lily was holding her calendar and she accidentally dropped it on the floor. Her mommy heard the sound and asked, "What happened, Lily?" "I dropped my calendar," Lily said. Her mommy picked up the calendar and saw that it was cold outside. "It's a cold day today, Lily. We should wear our warm coats when we go outside," her mommy said. Lily nodded and put on her coat. They went outside and had a fun day playing in the snow. When they came back inside, they had some hot cocoa to warm up. Lily looked at her calendar and smiled. Once upon a time there was a little girl who was sad and lonely. She had no one to play with and no one to talk to. She began to feel so sad that she started to weep. The little girl looked around for something that might cheer her up. Suddenly she spotted a tray of cookies sitting on the table. She smiled and grabbed a cookie. She ate the cookie and felt better. Just then, she heard a noise coming from the corner of the room. It was a little mouse! The mouse was deaf and could not hear but she could see him. The little girl smiled and brought a mouse a cookie. The mouse was so happy that he ate the cookie and they became friends. The little girl was no longer lonely. She had found a new friend. From then on, they played games together and shared the cookies. Whenever she felt sad, the mouse was always there to cheer her up. They were the best of friends. And they swung them around on their hands. In some religions there are many gods. The Hindus worship many gods. This is called polytheism. They may or may not believe in a Supreme Being above the gods. Some polytheistic religions are Hinduism, Shinto, Taoism, Wicca and variants of Buddhism that syncronised with traditional folk religions it came into contact with. The belief that there is only one god is called monotheism. People who only believe in one god usually write God with an uppercase first letter. Some monotheistic religions are Christianity, Judaism, Islam, Bah ' Faith and Sikhism. In English the word "gods" is written in lowercase letters. "God" is usually written with an uppercase letter when it refers to the Supreme Being. Some polytheists also like to use uppercase when talking about their gods.
